name: Robledo de Chavela and Fresnedillas Bunker Loop
name_original: Al búnker de Fresnedillas desde Robledo — Sierra de Guadarrama
description: This difficult 21.9-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Guadarrama features historical bunkers and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Robledo De Chavela is characterized by its diverse and well-preserved natural landscapes within the Sierra Oeste of Madrid, nestled between the Sierra de Guadarrama and Sierra de Gredos mountain ranges. The terrain features granite mountainous alignments, including peaks like Cerro de la Almenara (1259m) and San Benito peak (1626m), alongside extensive pine forests and holm oak groves. Rivers such as the Cofio and Alberche enrich the area, which is largely environmentally protected. Best mountain bike trails around Robledo De Chavela The most popular mountain bike trail is Robledo de Chavela and Fresnedillas Bunker Loop, a 21.9 miles (35.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete, passing historical bunkers. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Zarzalejo Village Center – Navahonda Hermitage loop from Robledo de Chavela, a difficult 39.1 miles (62.9 km) path, featuring technical sections and panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Navahonda Hermitage – Río Alberche loop from Robledo de Chavela, a 32.4 miles (52.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with a double ascent to the Navahonda Hermitage, often completed in about 4 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Robledo De Chavela is defined by granite mountainous alignments, extensive pine forests, and river valleys, offering routes for various skill levels. The network provides options for both challenging rides requiring advanced skills and routes accommodating any fitness level. The routes in Robledo De Chavela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Robledo De Chavela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Robledo De Chavela?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Robledo De Chavela, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 700 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Robledo De Chavela?
A: The terrain in Robledo De Chavela is characterized by granite mountainous alignments, extensive pine forests, holm oak groves, and river valleys. You'll encounter varied surfaces, from technical sections to flowing paths, often with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Robledo De Chavela?
A: While there are no trails specifically rated as 'easy', there are 6 routes considered 'moderate' difficulty. The majority of trails (34) are rated 'difficult', suggesting that many routes require good fitness and advanced riding skills.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in Robledo De Chavela?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5320106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II – Hermitage of Our Lady of Grace loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt;, which is 28.3 miles (45.5 km) long and features significant climbs. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32053788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde Pass – Zarzalejo Village Center loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt;, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) route with fast sections and technical zones.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Robledo De Chavela?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Robledo De Chavela are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352299962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo de Chavela and Fresnedillas Bunker Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6931548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarzalejo Village Center – Navahonda Hermitage loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31689978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navahonda Hermitage – Río Alberche loop&lt;/a&gt;, all designed to bring you back to your starting point.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Robledo De Chavela is rich in historical and natural landmarks. You can encounter historical bunkers on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352299962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo de Chavela and Fresnedillas Bunker Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5320106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical granite viewpoint offering views of the Monastery of El Escorial and the Guadarrama Valley. The Hermitage of Navahonda is also a frequent highlight on several routes, providing spectacular views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Robledo De Chavela?
A: The mountain bike trails in Robledo De Chavela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Riders often praise the diverse and well-preserved natural landscapes, the varied terrain, and the challenging routes that offer both technical sections and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or points of interest near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is nestled between the Sierra de Guadarrama and Sierra de Gredos mountain ranges, with over 85% of its territory environmentally protected. You can find several natural attractions, including various &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1620333/peaks-around-robledo-de-chavela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;mountain peaks&lt;/a&gt; like Cerro de la Almenara and San Benito peak, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3818696/lakes-around-robledo-de-chavela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;lakes&lt;/a&gt; such as Picadas Reservoir and Romeral Reservoir. The area is also a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), making it excellent for birdwatching.

Q: Is Robledo De Chavela a good destination for mountain biking in winter?
A: Robledo De Chavela's mountainous terrain means that winter conditions can vary. While lower elevations might be rideable, higher peaks like San Benito (1626m) could experience colder temperatures and potentially snow or ice. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before planning a winter ride.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail are not guaranteed, Robledo De Chavela and nearby villages like Zarzalejo, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6931548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarzalejo Village Center – Navahonda Hermitage loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer amenities where you can find refreshments and food. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Robledo De Chavela?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Robledo De Chavela, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher altitudes.
tips: Narrow path between Robledo de Chavela and the hermitage of Navahonda. It does not present technical complications and offers spectacular views.
The existence of a disappeared medieval town called Navahonda has led researchers to think that this hermitage was the Church of said town.

In Fresnedillas de la Oliva there is a very original and fun route that runs through the streets of the town: the route is called ''Casas con Vida''. In the Fresnedillas tourist office you can find a brochure with the marked route - on the route you will see a total of 86 paintings and a large mural (painted by the artist Elena Parlange). Some of the most beautiful images are located in hidden places that must be discovered.

Bunker of the rebel side in position "Cota 960" during the Spanish Civil War, this structure has five marksman posts and a covered machine gun nest, all with very good visibility at the time (the current vegetation was not there). These were connected by a single covered gallery with a single entrance and several shooting loopholes.
Fresnedillas was under Franco's rule since the fall of 1936.
Nice rock formation. There are stairs that allow you to climb to enjoy the view
Very picturesque track and in perfect condition. It is not technically difficult and it is a good option to avoid any trials with loose rock and comfortably reach Zarzalejo.

This is a very tough ascent that will make more than one set foot on the ground.
On the subsequent descent, we must be extremely careful because of the many steps where we will have to stop.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as a "hard mountain bike ride" and is not suitable for beginners. It requires good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for demanding sections where dismounting and pushing your bike might be necessary.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied and challenging. Given its 'hard' rating, you can expect technical sections with roots, rocks, and uneven surfaces that demand precise bike handling and good suspension. Mountain biking trails in the Sierra de Guadarrama often include very rocky and loose sections, steep ascents, and challenging descents.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll encounter the significant Fresnedillas Civil War Bunker, which provides a glimpse into Spain's past. Other notable spots include the Navahonda Hermitage, a unique Casas con Vida mural in Fresnedillas de la Oliva, and an interesting Rock Formation with Stairs in the Dehesa de Robledo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Robledo de Chavela?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, Robledo de Chavela is the starting point. You should look for public parking areas within the town, especially near the beginning of the route, to ensure convenient access to the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Guadarrama region offers beautiful scenery, but weather conditions can vary. Generally, spring and autumn provide the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring cold temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, making some sections more challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs in the Sierra de Guadarrama can vary. While many trails allow dogs, they are typically required to be on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local signage for specific rules in the immediate area of the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain bike trail or most areas within the Sierra de Guadarrama for recreational activities like mountain biking. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations and private property. Some specific natural reserves or protected areas within the broader park might have different rules, but this route typically does not require special permissions.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the "De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde San Lorenzo del Escorial a Pelayos de la Presa)", "Camino del Molino Patatero", and the "GR 10". It also crosses sections of the "Camino de Zarzalejo a San Martín de Valdeiglesias" and "Camino del Pinar de Navahonda".

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking scenery and a privileged natural environment. The region offers views of rolling hills and dense forests, along with panoramic vistas over the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range. The presence of historical Civil War bunkers also adds a unique and thought-provoking element to the landscape.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail in Robledo de Chavela or Fresnedillas de la Oliva?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Robledo de Chavela and Fresnedillas de la Oliva are towns with amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and possibly small shops in these villages to refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check their operating hours, especially if you're riding during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes/patch kit), a first-aid kit, and appropriate protective gear. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the route downloaded is also highly recommended, as some sections can be remote. Dress in layers suitable for varying mountain weather.
